posts.author: RAJ_THARUN
posts.text: DFPCL contract is based on Brent crude + NG TTF Brent don’t increase like TTF because we can store Crude but it is difficult to store LNG, you need to have infrastructure to store it at -160degC. Due to this TTF prices will go up 3-4 times during crisis, but brent will go up like 50-60%. As the contract is based on Brent + NG TTF, Deepak will get it at discounted price.

posts.author: Avin
posts.text: what is the source of you saying that the LNG is at a discounted price? The agreement for oil and gas purchases esp long term gas purchases are always linked to benchmark plus a premium plus freight (normally the prevailing freight rates). If you have concrete information about “discounted” LNG then that is very unique and huge advantage to the company. PS: I work in oil and gas sector. No positions/holdings in Deepak Fertilizer.

posts.author: RAJ_THARUN
posts.text: Now while others struggle for LNG due to closure of Strait of Hormuz. Deepak Fert will have access via Norway at a discounted price.

posts.author: Kamal_Bhuptani
posts.text: CCPL was acquired by DMSL a subsidiary of Deepak fertilisers for ~ 121 cr in Dec’25. CCPL has no turnover for the last 3 years still the company paid big amt for the acquisition. It was mainly for the licenses and the expertise in explosives that ccpl has. Deepak fertilisers can now thru DMSL export the explosive chemistry for mining to DMSL’s Australian subsidiary. Also they can now increase their exports to rest of the world with this newly acquired expertise. Disc : Not sebi registered . Not a buy or sell recommendation

posts.author: vikashkota
posts.text: Any members explain why the company acquired Chardham Chemicals Private Limited (“CCPL”)? d1bf0fa7-af78-411f-b3f0-65e7471c4b22.pdf (289.5 KB) No reportable revenue
